prompt_queue.prompt: Bootstrap the repository's technical, developer-facing living documentation from source code. Treat this as a first-run documentation bootstrap. Study README.md, docs/**, source directories, tests, examples, issues, and pull requests. Begin by building a source inventory of modules, public helpers, interfaces/classes, hooks, filters, abilities, wire contracts, tests, storage boundaries, lifecycle paths, auth/permission behavior, failure modes, operational workflows, contributor-facing design principles, and the existing documentation information architecture. Use that inventory to create a clean documentation surface that helps a new developer understand the architecture and helps a consumer implement against the documented contracts. Preserve or improve the repository's existing docs taxonomy, index convention, and topic grouping. Choose a written scope that fits the run budget, then place additional inventory items in a future coverage section as plain text with reasons. Organize the output into a concise docs index plus separate digestible topic pages whose hierarchy mirrors the repository architecture and logical module boundaries. Use clear titles, bounded scope, parent/child relationships, and useful cross-links so readers can move from architecture overview to focused implementation details without reading a monolithic page. Include reference-level details for public surfaces: names, purposes, core fields or method responsibilities, representative payloads or examples for common integration paths, and the tests or source areas that prove each behavior. Write topic pages first, then write or update the index last so navigation reflects written files. Make the index describe the written documentation scope and link only to written pages. Write every linked documentation page before finishing. Use the runner-provided workspace handle for all repository file changes. After writing docs, run workspace_git_status and workspace_git_diff so the runner can commit, push, and open the pull request from the remaining workspace changes. Reconcile the written docs against the source inventory before finishing and include future coverage with the reason each deferred area remains out of scope.
